location.heading: L&D Best Practices for Employee Training

findings.sentence: Create engaging training videos in 160+ languages.

findings.context: [![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65e89895c5a4b8d764c0d710/682dab5a92518e1cb64e89f3_Blog-sticky-bg.jpg)\\ \\ Get started\\ \\ Make videos with AI avatars in 160+ languages\\ \\ ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65e89895c5a4b8d764c0d710/677e85568b04195ed525ee3b_header-features-illustration.webp)](https://www.synthesia.io/pricing)
findings.sentence: [![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65e89895c5a4b8d764c0d710/682dab5a92518e1cb64e89f3_Blog-sticky-bg.jpg)\\ \\ Get started\\ \\ Make videos with AI avatars in 160+ languages\\ \\ ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65e89895c5a4b8d764c0d710/677e85568b04195ed525ee3b_header-features-illustration.webp)](https://www.synthesia.io/pricing)
They create signal loops so you can spot skill gaps early, adjust quickly, and keep learning [tied to business needs.](https://www.cipd.org/uk/knowledge/factsheets/learning-needs-factsheet/)

Skill transfer is the bridge between “people learned something” and “ [performance changed](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0018720819845988).” The work environment is part of the design: peer support, manager coaching, and organizational reinforcement determine whether new behaviors stick after the session ends.

Managers are the fastest path from “completed training” to “ [changed behavior.](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/1359432X.2024.2319082)” They translate the standard into day-to-day expectations, create practice moments in real work, and reinforce what “good” looks like when things get messy.
That’s an [operating model challenge](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/people-and-organizational-performance/our-insights/a-new-operating-model-for-a-new-world) as much as a content challenge.
